payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: By the time you go to stop him, three of the servants that have grabbed a few of your belongings and been carrying away, are swung at, Craven Edge passing through, and their energy dispersing, and their contents of whatever they're carrying drop and clink to the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What the fuck.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Hi. I step in after her. Yes, I've made some changes to the place. Living quarters are upstairs, there's a kitchen, parlor, game room over there, billiards, all sorts of stuff. We have a music room. There are a hundred attendants who can wait on us. I've assigned some of them specific jobs. Five are going to play music for us wherever we want, especially during our meals.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Let me take your things, Master."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yes, yes, here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I come inside and I see the ethereal things, I'm like, ghosts! And I take Craven Edge and I swing at the ethereal sumbitch!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you attacking my attendants?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, the ghosts in your house!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They're my people!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It takes a minute, so yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is the door protected? Can anybody come in, or is it only if you invite them?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Only invited.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: This is the best fucking mansion ever. How-- I mean-- what?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How many times can you do this?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Once a day, but that's all we'll need, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Shit, yeah. Wait, wait, wait, so if we were in a fight and things went really south--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We could take a break.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps) No, but they could run away, it's not like time freezes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It does take a while to cast.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: They all look at each other. "Right away, sir." And all five of them drift off. A moment later, they all return with a spectral cow.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you have any rooms with very high ceilings?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The front.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The very front?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The front hall.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But I mean somewhere I could put a bed for myself and Trinket with very high ceilings.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, yes, there's a whole basement area. It's a large space.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wonderful. I'll move my bed down there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's for training and working with weapons.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That'll be perfect. Trinket needs to train.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'll go down there and I'll stay with Trinket.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I want to train a little, want to work out a bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Totally. I understand.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yep. I need a target, though. Hey, can you call one of those little ghostly guys over?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You can have five of them.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, right, fine. The first one of you that brings me a cow wins.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You do. And you take a few minutes to figure out the pitch and wobble of it, and it's less of a directional flight, more than it is a combination of how you hold the front of the broom and concentrate on its direction. But nevertheless, a few moments pass, and you get the hang of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yay, this is awesome! I'm really high!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I instruct the band to start playing Flight of the Bumblebee.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (whistling)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my gosh! Can I take my hands off?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do, and you start going, "Whoa!" Go ahead and make a dexterity check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my God. I probably should have been lower to the ground when I said that. Okay, that's fine. Oh, I'm still exhausted!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's fine. You're fine. A really bad roll might have had a circumstance, but you manage to catch yourself on the broom in time and, okay. You're getting the hang of it. It's still not something you're used to, but it's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I point it down towards the ground and get lower?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You do. And you take a few minutes to figure out the pitch and wobble of it, and it's less of a directional flight, more than it is a combination of how you hold the front of the broom and concentrate on its direction. But nevertheless, a few moments pass, and you get the hang of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yay, this is awesome! I'm really high!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I instruct the band to start playing Flight of the Bumblebee.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (whistling)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my gosh! Can I take my hands off?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do, and you start going, "Whoa!" Go ahead and make a dexterity check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my God. I probably should have been lower to the ground when I said that. Okay, that's fine. Oh, I'm still exhausted!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's fine. You're fine. A really bad roll might have had a circumstance, but you manage to catch yourself on the broom in time and, okay. You're getting the hang of it. It's still not something you're used to, but it's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I point it down towards the ground and get lower?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. You all eventually find your ways to your rooms, happy and comfortable in the fact that no one has to take watch for this night. As you all get to bed, a few hours into the evening, Keyleth, you are just starting to doze off when you hear a knock at the door.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, guys, it didn't work, you can go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They all get up. The music stops. And they all slowly drift out of the room, one by one. Fireplace still crackling.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's nice in here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm sitting wherever there's a lounge of any kind. I'm working in the sketchbook. Working on things.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not a worry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll be reading my alchemy book, because I have had so little time to catch up on my reading.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We never talk anymore, do we?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We never do.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Mmm.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm so happy! It's the best!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So good!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to try to sleep. It's going to be really, really hard because I'm so excited, but I really need to get some rest!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did you feed the dog?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Today? I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, anyone want to do anything else? You going to go rest for the evening?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm good.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He goes over and takes one of the nearby chairs and sets it down, places one across from him for you to sit, gestures with his one arm. Sits down. "I would not speak with those I do not trust. And I have seen your purity of soul, Keyleth. So I come to you with these concerns." His eyes trail off for a moment in the distance, and he gathers his thoughts and his words. "Four years ago, a young woman came to Pyrah for redemption. A lost girl, cast out and wishing for acceptance. We, as we do with outcasts, took her in, taught her. And in time, called her one of our own, one of the Pyrah. In recent months, she began to grow distant, taking to staying in the Cindergrove unattended for days at a time. One afternoon, the mountain moved, angry with shifting fires. We felt a disturbance in the Gate, and when we went to investigate, there she was, commanding powers we did not know she had."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps awake) Scanlan?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You open the door?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: On my door?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: On your door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Who is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Keyleth, it is Cerkonos. Please open up. I wish to speak with you."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I open the door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Cerkonos comes in. The first thing you notice-- previously, you had seen what was his burned-off arm at the shoulder. You can now see the flesh is extended and is currently very slowly in the process of regenerating his lost arm. And it catches you off-guard for a moment, and he puts his hand up and says, "Sorry, it is not very presentable." And he still keeps his hand over it, slightly embarrassed by the mid-regenerative state of it. "I'm sorry to have disturbed you, I come as unease has taken my mind." And he enters the room and closes the door behind him. "May we sit?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Of course, please, Cerkonos.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I just do not wish you to make the same mistake I did. Anyway, I thank you for your companions and you coming to our aid. It shall not be forgotten."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "When she came to us she couldn't be more than 15."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So she's 19, 20?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Perhaps. She did not seem to blossom into womanhood as many do. We considered her more of a runt of her litter, if you will."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And what was her race?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "She seemed human."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She seemed human? What do you mean?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We take many forms as druids. If she was indeed human, at that age, to command such power... I do not know her true nature."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What was her name?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Her name was Raishan. Anyway. I wish to tell you this because my heart is heavy with guilt, and I want you to know that even in places of comfort, family, be careful, keep those you know you can trust close. Be mindful of those that promise aid when they have not proven themselves. If you have an instinct, follow it."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are you referring to something in particular, Cerkonos?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You sleep, but uneasily, as all of these realizations swirl in your head. Eventually sleep takes you, but it is not an easy sleep. Just enough to function. Barely. Vax. As you yourself have attempted to stave off sleep for many days, pushing yourself to your limits, it finally takes its toll, and you drift off into a deep, deep slumber. In that slumber, you find yourself in darkness. Echoes of a distant voice, muffled, as if underwater. Crash. The darkness is shattered into a thousand black feathers, and you find yourself falling, falling. Falling towards what seems to be a tangled net of glowing threads. You're afraid you might impact at such a speed, but before you do, you slow and come to a stop before this extremely prominent glowing weave, this infinite tapestry in all directions. You are uncertain of the meaning of this moment when a large cold hand reaches past your shoulder and grasps a thread right before your face, this singular glowing thread, pulsing with light like a heartbeat. You follow the arm over your shoulder, and glance up directly into the massive form of the Raven Queen: porcelain face like a mask, long jet-black hair, her body encasing you like a physical cage. Your eyes follow the arm back to the thread that she carries between her massive fingers. She tugs it, ever so gently, and it causes waves to ripple through the tapestry into infinity. Her voice fills your mind, both calm but cold. "This is you, my champion. You are fate-touched. The choice is yours. Rebirth or ruin. Maker or martyr. Conqueror, tyrant, or nothing." She plucks the thread from the weave, snapping it. You plummet into the hole left behind it, falling faster and faster into the dark. Losing control, you turn around and see the Raven Queen, disappearing into the distance, growing smaller and smaller, until suddenly, nothing. Grog. You fall into a deep snore, heavy, heavy snore, after drinking the wine, climbing up walls, you've had an exhausting day of--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you, Cerkonos. And I'm glad that you're still with us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Kaitiaki, Keyleth."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Kaitiaki, Cerkonos.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cerkonos leaves.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And I start poring through my notes for the name Raishan, because Keyleth believes she has heard that name before as one of the dragons, correct? One of the white--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) It's the green one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: --the green one. Okay, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That is true.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do I have any other reference, would I know anything else about that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's all you know, is the name Raishan was used when the Conclave attacked Emon. And it seemed the name belonged to the green dragon that initiated the assault where you guys were.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fucking bitch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, these thoughts echoing through your mind--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Streets will run green with revenge!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, they will!

payload.raw_text: The light subsides and your vision returns. You look to see hundreds of people dead at your feet. Farmers, children. You see Pike, motionless, cast among the carnage. You want to scream, but instead you laugh, against your own judgment and intent, like you're locked within yourself and have no control. And as you look up from the pile of corpses at your feet, you see a towering figure before you, cast in furs and bone, standing even a head and a half's height above you, is Kevdak, looking down at you with pride. The entire herd steps up beside him, staring at you atop your throne of corpses, and the herd kneels. Kevdak bows, looking up at you, smiling. "You. You have finally made me proud, Grog." You wake up (gasp), spittle and sand and grit mashed against your face. Who knows how long it's been, but it's been a while since you've had a dream, let alone one like that. Now, as you all eventually come to in the morning, having your evening's rest, you've recovered your hit points, your spells, all of the usual good stuff, in a fantastic mansion. Maybe not the most fantastic rest for some of you, but a fantastic mansion. You all eventually find your way to the breakfast nook, which is as extravagant as you would imagine a Scanlan-based breakfast nook would be.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: You sense others around you, closing in suddenly. A voice, ever-whispering in your ear, says, "Release me." Still blinded, eyes closed, on instinct, you pull the cold, dark blade from your back and swing wide, over and over again, feeling the shuddering shock of each impact, your strength matched by the razor-like edge of your weapon, cleaving down any who would approach. The high of adrenaline is incredible, and you laugh aloud at the sheer thrill of your own power.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The light subsides and your vision returns. You look to see hundreds of people dead at your feet. Farmers, children. You see Pike, motionless, cast among the carnage. You want to scream, but instead you laugh, against your own judgment and intent, like you're locked within yourself and have no control. And as you look up from the pile of corpses at your feet, you see a towering figure before you, cast in furs and bone, standing even a head and a half's height above you, is Kevdak, looking down at you with pride. The entire herd steps up beside him, staring at you atop your throne of corpses, and the herd kneels. Kevdak bows, looking up at you, smiling. "You. You have finally made me proud, Grog." You wake up (gasp), spittle and sand and grit mashed against your face. Who knows how long it's been, but it's been a while since you've had a dream, let alone one like that. Now, as you all eventually come to in the morning, having your evening's rest, you've recovered your hit points, your spells, all of the usual good stuff, in a fantastic mansion. Maybe not the most fantastic rest for some of you, but a fantastic mansion. You all eventually find your way to the breakfast nook, which is as extravagant as you would imagine a Scanlan-based breakfast nook would be.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Phillip! Phillip!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You sense others around you, closing in suddenly. A voice, ever-whispering in your ear, says, "Release me." Still blinded, eyes closed, on instinct, you pull the cold, dark blade from your back and swing wide, over and over again, feeling the shuddering shock of each impact, your strength matched by the razor-like edge of your weapon, cleaving down any who would approach. The high of adrenaline is incredible, and you laugh aloud at the sheer thrill of your own power.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (laughs triumphantly)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The light subsides and your vision returns. You look to see hundreds of people dead at your feet. Farmers, children. You see Pike, motionless, cast among the carnage. You want to scream, but instead you laugh, against your own judgment and intent, like you're locked within yourself and have no control. And as you look up from the pile of corpses at your feet, you see a towering figure before you, cast in furs and bone, standing even a head and a half's height above you, is Kevdak, looking down at you with pride. The entire herd steps up beside him, staring at you atop your throne of corpses, and the herd kneels. Kevdak bows, looking up at you, smiling. "You. You have finally made me proud, Grog." You wake up (gasp), spittle and sand and grit mashed against your face. Who knows how long it's been, but it's been a while since you've had a dream, let alone one like that. Now, as you all eventually come to in the morning, having your evening's rest, you've recovered your hit points, your spells, all of the usual good stuff, in a fantastic mansion. Maybe not the most fantastic rest for some of you, but a fantastic mansion. You all eventually find your way to the breakfast nook, which is as extravagant as you would imagine a Scanlan-based breakfast nook would be.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Phillip! Phillip!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You sense others around you, closing in suddenly. A voice, ever-whispering in your ear, says, "Release me." Still blinded, eyes closed, on instinct, you pull the cold, dark blade from your back and swing wide, over and over again, feeling the shuddering shock of each impact, your strength matched by the razor-like edge of your weapon, cleaving down any who would approach. The high of adrenaline is incredible, and you laugh aloud at the sheer thrill of your own power.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (laughs triumphantly)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. So you're going to the Crossroads. The Crossroads is about two days of travel northeast of the Frostweald, about three days' travel.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The closest destination. Would the Crossroads be good? Maybe get us to the Crossroads?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Or we could just go to Westruun. Is Westruun still there?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Westruun's under dragon rule. There's an actual dragon on Westruun.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not on. Up in the mountain, but watching down, probably.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But they've got wyverns policing, right, in Westruun?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You didn't notice in your scry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to assume. Let's do the Crossroads.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's do it! Let's do it! Let's do it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We could theoretically run into your herd, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I mean, I hope we do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: To the Crossroads!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You complete the spell against one of the petrified trees on the outside of the Cindergrove. The doorway opens up, and you guys can see a thicket of Bramblewood trees on the opposite side. You all rush through one after the other, Trinket jumping behind. You all make it through, and you're now standing on the central pathway of the Bramblewood. Further south, you're probably about an hour and a half foot travel before you exit the southern portion of the forest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! I remember that!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We spent a whole day in that park.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep. You begin to cast the spell and you concentrate on that tree in the park. There's no connection to the tree. You gather that the tree may not have survived the initial attack on Westruun. However, you do take a moment and you do recall at the entrance to the Bramblewood, when you guys were briefly attacked by that group of thieves that were pulling you off to the side, pretending to be a stranded caravan.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That was good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There was a series of rather large trees that marked the roadway there, and you recall one that had distinct markings, and you do manage to find a point of concentration or connection there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I guess it took me a moment to find signal. Okay. All right. We're good.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You need a signal boost.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think I got one.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: To the Frostweald? You're about a day and a half. On horseback you're about a day, under a day. On foot, you're looking at about two days' travel, a little over two days' travel.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I guess it took me a moment to find signal. Okay. All right. We're good.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You need a signal boost.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think I got one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You complete the spell against one of the petrified trees on the outside of the Cindergrove. The doorway opens up, and you guys can see a thicket of Bramblewood trees on the opposite side. You all rush through one after the other, Trinket jumping behind. You all make it through, and you're now standing on the central pathway of the Bramblewood. Further south, you're probably about an hour and a half foot travel before you exit the southern portion of the forest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All right, sorry, guys, this is as close as I could get us.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, this is great!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And how far are we now? A day's travel? Two days' travel, you said?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "About half a day ago. Dusk yesterday. I don't know. We all stay inside around that time just in case."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Then why did they take your daughter?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't want to think about it."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: To keep him working!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Look. Say we were really bored, and we wanted to get you your family back. Where would you go?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He points over to the city. "But there's one right out..." And you see him pointing angrily around the corner, probably to the one that you saw on the outskirts of the farm area you guys came down in.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let's go to the city then, I suppose.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, what?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: There's a dragon!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: To do what, to attack half-orcs that work for a dragon?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Farmer. What's your name?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Reginald."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Reginald. Reginald, we want to help. How often do you see the dragon flying around?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Once every couple days, maybe."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Once every couple days. When was the last time you saw him?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I haven't seen anything flying but the dragon. There was a huge fight not more than a week ago between these people and that thing. They seem to have come to an accord."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He points over to the city. "But there's one right out..." And you see him pointing angrily around the corner, probably to the one that you saw on the outskirts of the farm area you guys came down in.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let's go to the city then, I suppose.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, what?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: There's a dragon!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: To do what, to attack half-orcs that work for a dragon?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Farmer. What's your name?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Reginald."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Reginald. Reginald, we want to help. How often do you see the dragon flying around?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Once every couple days, maybe."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Once every couple days. When was the last time you saw him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "About half a day ago. Dusk yesterday. I don't know. We all stay inside around that time just in case."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does he have patrols? Does he have wyverns working for him as well, that fly around?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "All of them kept going. We thought we were safe. We tried to re-gather. And then, within a day, a bunch of these bastards came and took what was left. Slaughtering, stealing."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: These people?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The orcs?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The orcs, the whatever they are. The big strong angry ones that keep taking all of our stuff."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, no.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What, what?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What do they look like?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do they look like him, maybe?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Some do."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, fuck me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fuck, fuck, fuck!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The dragon came in and tore everything down."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Damn it. Shit hammer.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Burned, liquefied, dissolved portions of this city, people screaming. And then it left. Both of them."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You'll be okay. We're going to keep going. Good luck.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, wait, wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Scanlan!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, we have to keep going. We can't face these goliaths and dragons and orc guards! This is craziness!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: One of the dragons was green?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "From what we saw."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Green and a black.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Green and a black. I think a white one, but that one kept going."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I mean, we'll be fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And the green one kept going?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: This is an individual that you knew quite a ways ago, a young goliath who was still about a half a foot shorter than you when you knew him. He was named Horace, because he hated it, and Kevdak gave him this name and you actually, at some point in the past, recall even giving Horace a good beating. A little of the smaller of the goliaths you've encountered. This is years later. This punk kid, and he was kind of a punk, has grown up now, and is still smaller than you by a size, but still seems to be every bit of an agitating little bitch you remember, from the way that he's treating these people.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And best we can tell, he's the only guard? The only one?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, there's two. There's two.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have an idea!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, he was about to tell us who it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: From what you can see, you actually recognize this individual.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fucking nipple tassels!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Okay. We were heading up north. We took out a small caravan just south of the road cuts, near the Silvercut Way, and all of a sudden out of the sky, we saw these streaking dragons come through. Largest I've ever seen. Dangerous as the day's long. So we fled. We watched them come here and tear into this city. Rend it, destroy it, smoke and flames and ice and all sorts of craziness. And they kept going. We figured, dragons come, didn't take the spoils, there's plenty of spoils for us. More than we could ever dream of. Most of it's pretty well-guarded, but there couldn't be much left over there. So we gathered up who we had, and mind you, the herd's swelled a bit since you left, my friend. We conquered another wandering tribe last year. Zanror took the head of their leader and commanded them to join. We're about a hundred strong these days."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Right, listen. I was going to pounce on this poor farmer over here, but I saw you were obviously keeping your eye on him. Why are you doing that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Which one are you talking about? Reggie?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Old Broken Foot over here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) "He's a little bitch, that one is. No, but you know what? I caught him sniffing around. I think he's keeping some gold hidden."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Really? Gold? This poor farmer?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Twisted his arm a couple of times."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Have you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We could do this for old times' sake. Each take an arm, pull until he tells us."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, like wishbone?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that does sound like a good idea. Here's my thing. Why are you guys in this city? It's not really our thing to be in civilized places.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Here's the thing. Let me get loose here for a minute. I'm feeling a bit uncomfortable."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, I don't think I will, Horace.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "It's been probably half a day since breakfast. Aye. Why?" His expression begins to shift as realization begins to dawn on his face.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, it sounds really good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You've missed out, and there's quite a few openings now, since we've lost a few of the less than strong folk. You look strapping. I'm sure Kevdak would be more than happy to have you back."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I do. I've actually brought a few gifts.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) I notch an arrow.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I unsheathe Craven Edge.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's a good blade."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Isn't it. And I ask out loud, are you hungry?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As the stalks unwind, leaving his defiled body and you draw the blade free, Scanlan, you find, across the body, there is a simple, slightly chipped-but-still-functional greatsword that was sheathed in the back, wrapped in a haphazard hide that functioned as a scabbard. Mostly just leather pants, a few furs, looks to be a bone necklace and a bone bracelet around the wrist which looks to be teeth, human teeth. About 27 gold pieces in total change.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You wanted to?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, he was going to go tell about us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You were conscious of that?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is it me?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're using too-big words.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So listen, right? Either we stay and do some recon, or we take out the other guy that's walking back to the city. Really shouldn't be a problem. But if they're all in there, 50 strong, I don't think we're quite ready for that. The sneaky fucks in our group could go take a look, if you're feeling randy.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What would be our mission?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Info only.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You killed this one quite easily.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Very easily.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, but that's because we did it as a group.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did we?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Could we wait until nightfall?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to check out his body. See if there's any booty on his body.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, as soon as we move, we won't be camouflaged in this crop anymore. We'll lose that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we won't look like crops.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: His structure is still standing as well. A few buildings around did not fare so well, but his shop and a number of other ones adjacent to it seem to still be standing. You can't really tell if they're functional, or how busy they are, or if anyone's doing anything with them, but that's still there. You do notice, though, at numerous parts of the street, there are just piles and piles of burnt wood and ash, like they're large burn piles that were set at different periods of time and have long since burned. Little bits of white, post-burning plumes that still smolder over the past few days. You're not quite sure what these piles contained at the time they were set ablaze. There is still the worship district here. You have the various temples. Best you can glance, a few of them have been partially toppled. There is damage to some of the buildings. Still too far away to make out any specific details beyond that, some of the structures having taken a hit in previous conflicts. You're not sure from which source.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we see Wilhand anywhere?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this distance, no. You also note, towards the shopping district of Westruun, there is the shop of Abjurist Noja, one of your first enchanter friends who protected you during your first Winter's Crest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! He cast the protection spell on us!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This is making me feel very old.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The Margrave's. Towards the southern central portion of Westruun, south of the main town square where the bulk of your first Winter's Crest festival here in Westruun took place. The Margrave's house is at the very southernmost portion of that. That entire square is lined with row after row of sharpened wood sticks and various other weapons, tridents, broken swords that have been, with ropes, strapped together to form a military barricade. If anything were to attempt to attack from the ground, it would be difficult to do so if this area was filled with prepared soldiers, using these as protective guard. The actual Margrave's home is intact, although at this glance, you can see a lot of it has been defiled, elements of the outside walls are strewn with dark burgundy paint slapped over. You can see what looks to be some sort of strings adorning edges of it, like decoration. You see, walking through this area, what looks to be 12 or 13 or so armed individuals of varying heights. Probably a good six or seven goliaths among them. Others appear to be human, half-orc, it's hard to make out the full details from this distance. They're all on the outskirts surrounding what was previously the Margrave's home. Now the homestead of Kevdak, seemingly.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What about the mayor's house where we heard Horace--?
